snippets.title: ColumnTransformer: scale engineered numerics, encode categoricals
snippets.code:
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier

numeric_cols = ["tenure_days", "log_revenue", "sessions_per_week"]
cat_cols = ["plan_tier"]

num_pipe = Pipeline([
    ("impute", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
    ("scale", StandardScaler()),
])
cat_pipe = Pipeline([
    ("impute", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
    ("encode", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore")),
])

preprocess = ColumnTransformer([
    ("num", num_pipe, numeric_cols),
    ("cat", cat_pipe, cat_cols),
])

model = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocess),
    ("knn", KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=7)),
])
# model.fit(X_train, y_train) — scaler fits on train numerics only
snippets.explanation: Engineered numeric columns pass through impute → StandardScaler; categoricals encode without scaling. One outer fit learns all train-only statistics.

snippets.title: Tree pipeline: omit scaler on engineered numerics
snippets.code:
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer

numeric_cols = ["txn_count_7d", "amount_to_limit_ratio"]
cat_cols = ["merchant_category"]

num_pipe = Pipeline([("impute", SimpleImputer(strategy="median"))])
cat_pipe = Pipeline([
    ("impute", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
    ("encode", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore")),
])

preprocess = ColumnTransformer([
    ("num", num_pipe, numeric_cols),
    ("cat", cat_pipe, cat_cols),
])

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocess),
    ("rf", RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=200, random_state=0)),
])
# pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
snippets.explanation: For tree-only models, the numeric branch stops at imputation—no StandardScaler because split thresholds are scale-invariant on monotone transforms.

snippets.title: Ridge on engineered features with scale branch
snippets.code:
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer

engineered_numeric = ["log_revenue", "tenure_months", "support_tickets_per_year"]

ridge_pipe = Pipeline([
    ("impute", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
    ("scale", StandardScaler()),
    ("ridge", Ridge(alpha=1.0)),
])
# ridge_pipe.fit(X_train[engineered_numeric], y_train)
snippets.explanation: Ridge penalties apply fairly only when engineered columns share comparable variance; scaling sits immediately before the regularized estimator.

snippets.title: Cross-validation with engineered features (no leakage)
snippets.code:
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
import numpy as np

# X columns already engineered: age, log_spend, visits_per_month
X = np.column_stack([
    np.random.default_rng(0).integers(20, 60, 300),
    np.random.default_rng(1).normal(4, 1, 300),
    np.random.default_rng(2).poisson(3, 300),
])
y = (X[:, 1] > 4.5).astype(int)

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("scale", StandardScaler()),
    ("knn", KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=5)),
])
print("CV mean:", cross_val_score(pipe, X, y, cv=5).mean().round(3))
snippets.explanation: Wrapping the scaler inside Pipeline ensures each CV fold refits μ/σ on inner-train rows only—never on pre-scaled full matrices.
